title: Sautéed Green Beans and Onions
description: Green beans and caramelized onions sautéed together in olive oil or butter. Simple, fast, works alongside almost anything.

ingredients.name: lemon zest or grated parmesan to finish (optional)

instructions.text: Optional but recommended: blanch green beans in boiling salted water for 2–3 minutes, then transfer immediately to an ice water bath to stop cooking. Drain and set aside. This keeps the color vibrant and gives you more control over texture.
Heat olive oil or butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add sliced onions and cook, stirring occasionally, 5–7 minutes until softened and starting to caramelize.
If using garlic, stir it in and cook 30–60 seconds until fragrant.
Add green beans to the skillet. Season with salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes if using. Sauté 5–7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until beans are tender-crisp and coated in the onion mixture.
Transfer to a serving dish. Finish with lemon zest or grated parmesan if desired. Serve warm.

notes:
The blanching step is optional but worth doing -- it locks in the green color and gives you a head start on cooking so the beans don't spend too long in the pan.

For a vegan version, use olive oil instead of butter.
